output: Executed batch 2 tasks `T2` and `T6`. Dead library move callbacks were removed from the edited surface, `useDragCoordinator` was added with RAF-coalesced indicator updates, `npx tsc --noEmit` passed, and a checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/video-editor-timeline-well-20260325-1848/execution_batch_2.json`.
deviations: T6: added optional `clipOffsetX` to `DragCoordinator.update(...)` so the coordinator can preserve pointer-offset-aware position math when `useClipDrag` is migrated in a later batch.

task_updates.executor_notes: Removed `onActionMoveStart`, `onActionMoving`, and `onActionMoveEnd` from `useTimelineEditing`, the editor context surface, and `<Timeline>`. `onChange` is now explicitly resize-only sync and no longer checks `crossTrackActive`. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes, and `rg -n "onActionMove(Start|ing|End)"` returns no hits in the edited TimelineEditor/useTimelineEditing/useTimelineState surface.

Added `useDragCoordinator` with a stable `coordinator` API, `indicatorRef`, and `editAreaRef`. `update()` computes a shared `DropPosition`, converts it to `DropIndicatorPosition`, and coalesces `show()` calls behind `requestAnimationFrame`; `end()` cancels pending frames, hides the indicator, and clears `lastPosition`. The hook is now instantiated in `useTimelineState` (provider level), not in `TimelineEditor`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: `npx tsc --noEmit` passes after removing the dead move callbacks. `onChange` remains wired on `<Timeline>` and now performs resize-only synchronization without the old `crossTrackActive` early return.

`useDragCoordinator` uses RAF coalescing for indicator `show()` calls, exposes `coordinator`, `indicatorRef`, and `editAreaRef`, and is instantiated in `useTimelineState` rather than `TimelineEditor`. The next batch still needs to thread those refs through context and render `<DropIndicator>` from `TimelineEditor`.
